sources: docs/spec/ONT_ISSUANCE_FEE_MECHANICS.md §4 Recommended mechanics — 'F >= Sigma g_i' / 'mechanical from the batch contents'
docs/core/B2_KERNEL_HARDENING.md F1 + F6 step-2/step-3 correction (compute-from-committed-contents core is CITED)

inputs.attackFlagText: Sigma g_i is only computable once the batch contents are known: a fee verdict issued before the DA window resolves can be gamed by withholding bytes — the fee predicate must compose with the DA verdict, never run on self-declared totals.
inputs.predicateSignature: gateFeeValidation(anchorTxIntrinsicFee, committedBatchContents{N, perNameGateAmounts}, witnessedChainFacts, priorKernelState) -> {verdict, reason}

fixture.caseSelfDeclared: an anchor whose gate is evaluated against a self-declared / pre-DA-resolution batch total (not the committed, served batch contents) -> reject (the fee predicate must compose with committed contents + the DA verdict, never a self-declared total)
fixture.positiveControl: F >= Sigma g_i computed over the committed batch contents -> accept

inputs.scopeNote: Cited-now (candidate ONT_ISSUANCE_FEE_MECHANICS §4 + the F6 compute-from-committed-contents core, CITED in step-3): the kernel's posture against self-declared totals — the fee verdict binds to Sigma g_i over committed batch contents, never a publisher-declared/withheld total. DELIBERATELY EXCLUDES (B3): the per-name g(name) schedule itself. This vector asserts ONLY no-self-declared-totals composition.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: predicate-input shape proposed at candidate tier; reviewer sign-off complete; locked.

expected.invariant: a fee verdict computed against a self-declared / pre-DA total is rejected; the gate binds to F >= Sigma g_i over the committed batch contents and composes with the DA verdict
expected.rationale: The fail-closed composition requirement is derivable from the cited candidate text: F1 is a candidate-stays-grade rule whose core (fee verdict bound to F >= Sigma g_i over committed batch contents, no self-declared totals) is stated in ONT_ISSUANCE_FEE_MECHANICS §4/§8. A negative vector pinning that a fee verdict computed against a self-declared/withheld total is rejected (must compose with committed contents) is derivable now without inventing a rule. The g(name) schedule itself is B3, but the kernel's posture against self-declared totals is the F6 core, already CITED in step-3.

sources: docs/spec/ONT_ISSUANCE_FEE_MECHANICS.md §4 ('Sigma inputs - Sigma outputs') / §5 ('the fee is checked from Bitcoin, not asserted by anyone')
docs/core/B2_KERNEL_HARDENING.md F2 (verdict cited; proposed −test: missing prevout witness → no fee fact → MUST NOT count)

inputs.attackFlagText: The spec grounds the check in 'a full verifier already replays Bitcoin and holds the UTXO set' — that is I/O outside the pure kernel; B3 must deliver prevout-value witnesses as chain facts, and the predicate's behavior when a prevout witness is absent is specified nowhere (fail-closed is implied, not stated).
inputs.predicateSignature: gateFeeValidation(anchorTxIntrinsicFee from witnessed prevout values, committedBatchContents, witnessedChainFacts, priorKernelState) -> {verdict}

fixture.caseAbsentPrevout: an anchor with one input's prevout-value witness ABSENT -> no intrinsic fee fact (Sigma inputs - Sigma outputs) is derivable -> reject (fail-closed)
fixture.noSubstitution: the kernel MUST NOT substitute an asserted or zero fee when a prevout-value witness is missing

inputs.scopeNote: Cited-now (candidate ONT_ISSUANCE_FEE_MECHANICS §4/§5 — fee = Sigma in - Sigma out from witnessed Bitcoin facts, not asserted): the kernel's fail-closed posture on absent prevout-value evidence (a flag purely about fail-closed posture on absent evidence is vector-now per the gate carve-out). DELIBERATELY EXCLUDES (B3): the prevout-value witness wire FORMAT. This vector asserts ONLY no-fee-fact-on-absent-prevout -> reject.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: predicate-input shape proposed at candidate tier; reviewer sign-off complete; locked.

expected.invariant: when a prevout-value witness is absent, no intrinsic fee fact is derivable and the anchor is rejected; no asserted or zero fee is substituted
expected.rationale: The flag's actionable claim is the kernel's fail-closed posture on absent prevout-value evidence. Per the gate rules, a flag purely about fail-closed posture on absent evidence IS derivable now even though the prevout-value witness FORMAT is a B3 deliverable. F2 itself is CITED (intrinsic fee = Sigma inputs - Sigma outputs from witnessed facts, no asserted value). The proposed negative test (a prevout witness missing yields no fee fact, anchor MUST NOT count) is derivable from the system's no-oracle / fail-closed posture stated in §4/§5.

sources: docs/spec/ONT_ISSUANCE_FEE_MECHANICS.md §4 ('compute the anchor tx's exact fee (Sigma inputs - Sigma outputs)' / 'intrinsic to that one transaction')

inputs.attackFlagText: Out-of-band miner payment or CPFP child fees are not part of Sigma inputs − Sigma outputs of the anchor tx; the spec never states explicitly that only the anchor transaction's own intrinsic fee counts, leaving a lobbying surface ('the package paid enough').
inputs.predicateSignature: gateFeeValidation(anchorTxIntrinsicFee = Sigma(this tx inputs) - Sigma(this tx outputs), committedBatchContents, witnessedChainFacts, priorKernelState) -> {verdict}

fixture.caseCPFP: an anchor whose intrinsic fee < Sigma g_i, but a CPFP child / package transaction pays the difference -> reject (only the anchor tx's OWN intrinsic Sigma inputs - Sigma outputs counts)

inputs.scopeNote: Cited-now (candidate §4 'intrinsic to that one transaction'): only the anchor transaction's own intrinsic fee (Sigma of its own inputs minus its own outputs) counts toward the gate; CPFP child / package / out-of-band fees do not satisfy it. No invented rule.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: predicate-input shape proposed at candidate tier; reviewer sign-off complete; locked.

expected.invariant: a CPFP child's fee or any out-of-band payment cannot count toward the anchor's gate; only the anchor transaction's own intrinsic Sigma inputs - Sigma outputs satisfies F >= Sigma g_i
expected.rationale: F2 is CITED and explicitly grounds F as the anchor transaction's INTRINSIC fee (Sigma of the anchor tx's own inputs minus its own outputs). A negative vector that a CPFP child's fee or out-of-band payment cannot count toward the anchor's gate follows directly: the computed value is over the one anchor transaction's intrinsic inputs/outputs, and §4 states the fee is intrinsic to that one transaction so it can't be reused. No invented rule needed.

sources: docs/spec/ONT_ISSUANCE_FEE_MECHANICS.md §2 C1 ('Claiming k names must cost ~k*g, whether done as k solo claims or one batch of k')
docs/spec/ONT_ACQUISITION_STATE_MACHINE.md Claim ('Batching saves blockspace; it must not discount the anti-spam gate')

inputs.attackFlagText: A squatter batching a million names under one tx fee is the precise attack this kills; any future 'bulk pricing' parameter proposal violates constraint C1 by construction and must be unrepresentable in the predicate.

fixture.caseBulkDiscount: an anchor for N>1 names whose accepted fee = g + epsilon ('one gate covers the batch') -> reject
fixture.positiveBoundary: minimum accepted fee == N * g exactly (linear in N, no volume discount) -> accept

inputs.scopeNote: Cited-now (candidate §2 C1 + acquisition state-machine): the gate is per-name and batch-invariant — claiming k names costs ~k*g whether k solo anchors or one batch of k; batching may amortize ONLY blockspace, never the gate. Any per-name discount as batch size grows is rejected. No provisional dependency.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: predicate-input shape proposed at candidate tier; reviewer sign-off complete; locked.

expected.invariant: any fee schedule giving a per-name discount as batch size grows is rejected; the minimum valid fee is N * g (linear, batch-invariant); batching amortizes only blockspace
expected.rationale: F3 is CITED. Constraint C1 (claiming k names costs ~k*g whether k solo or one batch of k; batching may amortize only blockspace, never the gate) is explicit in §2 and the acquisition state machine. A negative vector that any fee schedule giving a per-name discount as batch size grows (bulk pricing) is rejected is directly derivable; the property test (min accepted fee == N*g, linear, no volume discount) is grounded. No provisional dependency, no missing rule.

sources: docs/spec/ONT_ISSUANCE_FEE_MECHANICS.md §5 ('N=1 is the un-censorable fallback'; self-sovereign I5)
docs/core/SOFTWARE_CANON.md Layer vocabulary / boundary rule ('pure predicate over witnessed inputs'; 'No DB, no network, no clock'; zero-I/O quarantine test) [ratified core, cited via T1]
docs/core/B2_KERNEL_HARDENING.md F8 verdict cited + proposed +test (no publisher-identity input in signature)

inputs.attackFlagText: If the predicate ever takes a publisher identity or endpoint as input, the censorship-resistance floor (invariant I5) silently breaks — the zero-I/O purity test should assert the predicate signature carries no source-identity parameter.
inputs.predicateSignature: gateFeeValidation(anchorFacts, committedBatchContents, fee) -> {verdict}  [NO publisher-identity / endpoint / source parameter]

fixture.kind: structural/architectural positive — discharged by the zero-I/O signature constraint
fixture.assertion: the gate-fee predicate's verdict is a function of (anchor facts, committed batch contents, fee) only; swapping the publisher identity / endpoint cannot change the verdict — there is no source-identity channel (N=1 self-posted and publisher-batched anchors validate by the identical rule)

inputs.scopeNote: Cited-now (candidate §5 N=1 un-censorable fallback / I5 + ratified SOFTWARE_CANON boundary rule via T1: pure predicate over witnessed inputs, zero-I/O): the gate predicate carries no publisher-identity/endpoint parameter; N=1 self-posted and publisher-batched anchors validate identically. Structural — discharged by the committed purity gate. DELIBERATELY EXCLUDES (B3): the g(name) schedule.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: structural vector; reviewer sign-off complete; locked.

expected.invariant: the gate-fee verdict is a function of (anchor facts, committed batch contents, fee) only; no publisher-identity/endpoint input can change it; publisher-batched and N=1 self-posted anchors validate by the identical rule (I5 censorship floor)
expected.rationale: F8 is CITED: the self-sovereign N=1 anchor is the identical rule, and §5 ties N=1 to the un-censorable fallback / I5 self-sovereign invariant. The zero-I/O purity test (predicate signature carries no source-identity parameter) is derivable from the ratified-core T1 boundary rule (pure predicate over witnessed inputs; no adapter/source judgment) and the SOFTWARE_CANON zero-I/O lock. No provisional dependency, no missing rule — a vector asserting the predicate has no publisher-identity input channel is authorable now.

sources: docs/core/STATUS.md line 53 ('Short-name opening bond (≤4 chars, mandatory bond-first — no cheap-claim path) ... 5+ chars use gate + contention', working baseline)

docs/GLOSSARY.md bond-first ('mandatory ... for names of 4 characters or fewer'); docs/core/B2_KERNEL_HARDENING.md F15 verdict cited + neededSpecWork ('threshold value confirms at launch-parameter freeze; parameterize the predicate on the threshold')

inputs.attackFlagText: The 4-char threshold is 'working baseline' (STATUS), not frozen — parameterize the predicate on the threshold.
inputs.predicateSignature: claimPathEligibility(name, thresholdT[launch-freeze], priorKernelState) -> {verdict}  -- name length <= T => bond-first only; > T => cheap-claim allowed

fixture.thresholdT: launch-freeze integer parameter (STATUS working baseline = 4), carried as a vector input — vectors run at two threshold values so no baked-in constant passes
fixture.negativeCompanion: a name of length <= T attempting to finalize via the cheap-claim path -> reject (must be bond-first)
fixture.positiveBoundary: a name of length T+1 finalizes via the cheap-claim path -> accept

inputs.scopeNote: Cited-now (candidate STATUS line 53 + GLOSSARY bond-first): the RULE — a name at-or-below the threshold takes the mandatory bond-first path (no cheap claim), above it the cheap-claim path is available. The threshold INTEGER is launch-freeze (parameterized, two values so no baked-in constant passes). DELIBERATELY EXCLUDES (spec-blocked): the in-batch-handling flag (F15-01). This vector asserts ONLY the threshold-parameterized path boundary.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: predicate-input shape proposed at candidate tier; threshold is launch-freeze param; reviewer sign-off complete; locked.

expected.invariant: a name of length <= threshold T cannot finalize via the cheap-claim path (bond-first only); a name of length T+1 may finalize via cheap claim; T is a launch-freeze parameter, not a baked-in constant
expected.rationale: The ≤4 threshold being a working baseline (not frozen) is a parameterization instruction, not a verdict ambiguity. The RULE (a name at-or-below the threshold takes the mandatory bond-first path; above it the cheap-claim path) is CITED and stable across the parameter; STATUS line 53 confirms the threshold semantics. A vector parameterized on the threshold (e.g. a name at length==threshold rejects the cheap path; length==threshold+1 may finalize via cheap claim) is authorable now, with the integer flagged as launch-freeze — no provisional decision, no missing rule. The behavior at the boundary (which side is bond-first) is determinate; only the integer value is open.
